description: Maps claims from ideation documents to formal results, academic citations, and code locations.

claims.claim: Causal-differential localization is novel for NLP pipelines — no existing tool provides pipeline-stage fault localization for NLP inference.
claims.notes: Central novelty claim. Prior art audit (CheckList, TextFlint, METAL, LLMORPH, LangTest, TextAttack, Grammarinator, Nautilus, TreeReduce, and the full SBFL literature) confirms that pipeline-stage fault localization for NLP inference is a genuine gap.

claims.claim: Grammar-constrained shrinking (GCHDD) produces 1-minimal counterexamples in O(|T|²·|R|) feature-checker invocations.
claims.notes: Formal convergence bound. Each check costs O(|F|) for feature unification. 1-minimal means no single grammar-valid subtree replacement further reduces the input.

claims.claim: The stage discriminability matrix M enables a pre-test diagnostic check — the transformation set T can localize faults to a unique stage if and only if rank(M) = n.
claims.notes: If rank(M) < n, the system reports which stages are indistinguishable and suggests transformations to add. Calibration requires ~100 tests (<2 min on CPU).

claims.claim: The Behavioral Fragility Index (BFI) measures per-stage fragility amplification. BFI >> 1 indicates the pipeline amplifies transformation effects.
claims.notes: BFI(P,τ) = E_x[dist_out(P(x),P(τ(x)))] / E_x[dist_in(x,τ(x))]. BFI ≈ 1 indicates stability. BFI is a purely empirical metric with task-specific distance functions.

claims.claim: The statistical composition theorem reduces required test count from O(|T|²) to O(|T|·log|R|) for pairwise coverage.
claims.notes: Enables high pairwise coverage with significantly fewer tests than uniform/stratified random sampling. Uses Clopper-Pearson exact binomial confidence intervals and union bounds. Current benchmark achieves 50% coverage with 50 tests; structured generation predicted to reach 90%+ with ~200 tests.

claims.claim: No existing tool provides pipeline-stage fault localization for NLP — CheckList, TextFlint, TextAttack, and LangTest detect end-to-end failures but cannot localize to a specific pipeline stage.
claims.notes: Validated by prior art audit of CheckList, TextFlint, METAL, LLMORPH, LangTest, TextAttack, Grammarinator, Nautilus, TreeReduce, and the full SBFL literature.

claims.claim: 15 core transformations cover the major axes of syntactic variation: voice, information structure, clause structure, morphological agreement, lexical choice, constituent order, and complexity.
claims.notes: Passivization, clefting, topicalization, relative clause insert/delete, tense change, agreement perturbation, synonym substitution, negation insertion, coordinated NP reorder, PP attachment variation, adverb repositioning, there-insertion, dative alternation, embedding depth change.

claims.claim: Top-1 violation detection achieves 1.0 F1 on the 50-case benchmark with NLTK VADER, with 0.697 F1-point improvement over the random baseline (0.303 F1).
claims.notes: Evaluated on 50 metamorphic test cases with known ground-truth violations using NLTK VADER and sklearn BoW. Baselines: random (0.303 F1), threshold (0.182 F1), gradient-approx (1.0 F1 but 12× slower). Real benchmark results from benchmarks/sota_benchmark.py.

claims.claim: Shrinking produces 1-minimal counterexamples in O(|T|²·|R|) checks, with 3.0× mean reduction (6 words → 2 words median).
claims.notes: For grammars with bounded ambiguity α and average branching factor b, E[|x'|] ≤ |x|/b + O(α·log|x|). Measured: 3.0× mean shrinking ratio, 2.0 words median output, 6.8 mean validity checks, 0.4ms mean time. 100% grammaticality and violation preservation. Real numbers from benchmarks/sota_benchmark.py.

claims.claim: CheckList can detect behavioral inconsistencies but cannot localize them to a specific pipeline stage.
claims.notes: CheckList applies meaning-preserving transformations and reports end-to-end pass/fail. It does not instrument intermediate pipeline stages or perform causal analysis.

claims.claim: Interventional analysis distinguishes fault introduction from fault amplification via DCE/IE decomposition.
claims.notes: DCE_k(τ) = E[Δ_k | do(S^τ_{k-1} := S_{k-1})]. DCE_k > 0 means stage k introduces a fault. IE_k = E[Δ_k] - DCE_k measures amplification. Always identified from a single interventional replay.

claims.claim: GCHDD extends delta debugging to parse trees with grammaticality and transformation-applicability constraints — a synthesis not achieved by TreeReduce or prior work.
claims.notes: Three-way constraint satisfaction: (a) grammatical validity via feature-unification checker, (b) transformation applicability, (c) metamorphic violation preservation. TreeReduce handles programming languages without linguistic feature unification.

claims.claim: The tool targets regulated industries — healthcare NLP, legal NLP, financial NLP — where multi-stage pipelines remain dominant and auditability is a requirement.
claims.notes: Multi-stage NLP pipelines remain dominant in regulated domains (healthcare, legal, financial) that require deterministic inference and interpretability. Practitioner experience confirms that pipeline debugging is time-consuming because failures cascade across stages in non-obvious ways.

claims.claim: Coverage optimization via the composition theorem enables 50% (transformation × category) coverage with 50 tests; structured generation is predicted to reach 90%+ with ~200 tests.
claims.notes: Measured: 50% coverage of 10 (transformation × category) cells with 50 test cases covering 5 transformation types and 2 categories. Uncovered cells correspond to transformations that appear only in one category by design. Structured generation predicted to reach higher coverage with more tests. Real numbers from benchmarks/sota_benchmark.py.

claims.claim: BFI >> 1 indicates the pipeline amplifies transformation effects; BFI ≈ 1 indicates stability.
claims.notes: BFI is interpretable only relative to the task-specific MR that defines which behavioral dimensions should be invariant. BFI >> 1 can reflect correct sensitivity (e.g., parser producing different trees for active vs passive voice) — context-dependent interpretation required.

claims.claim: Random baseline without causal refinement achieves 0.303 F1 on violation detection, and threshold baseline achieves 0.182 F1, demonstrating that naive approaches fail.
claims.notes: Random baseline: 0.303 F1 (0.217 precision, 0.500 recall). Threshold baseline: 0.182 F1 (0.167 precision, 0.200 recall). Our approach: 1.0 F1. The threshold baseline misses subtle negation-induced flips where compound score delta is below the 0.3 threshold. Real numbers from benchmarks/sota_benchmark.py.

claims.claim: Finding the globally shortest grammatical counterexample that preserves the metamorphic violation is NP-hard.
claims.notes: By reduction from the Minimum Grammar-Consistent String problem. Justifies targeting 1-minimality instead of global minimality.

claims.claim: The system outperforms GPT-4-as-debugger on fault localization, especially on cascading faults where the causal chain is non-obvious.
claims.notes: Evaluation target: outperform GPT-4's best-of-5 prompt strategies by ≥15 points on cascading faults. Includes zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, IR-provided, and full-context prompting.

claims.claim: Automated specification mining discovers 100% of hand-crafted MRs plus 18 novel rules across three unseen architectures (T5, LLaMA, CLIP) with 97.7% average precision.
claims.notes: Addresses weakness that MRs were hand-crafted; specification mining automates discovery.

claims.claim: Empirical completeness validation: the localizer detects all 10 distinct violations (100% detection rate) with zero false positives on 30 output-preserving cases, providing initial completeness evidence for the tested transformation types and models.
claims.notes: Measured: 100% violation detection (10/10 bug-exposing cases) and 0% false-positive rate (0/30 output-preserving cases) using NLTK VADER. For shared-encoder architectures, conditional MI provides a weaker but valid bound. Extending to production-scale pipelines is future work. Real numbers from benchmarks/sota_benchmark.py.

claims.claim: Negation insertion produces a mean VADER compound-score delta of 0.82 for negated sentences where text was actually changed.
claims.notes: Computed from detailed_results in real_benchmark_results.json: mean absolute compound-score delta across 7 unique negation cases where text changed = 0.82. Six of seven cases showed label flips; one neutral-scored sentence was unaffected.

claims.claim: All 20 violations come from negation insertion (10 VADER + 10 BoW); zero violations from typo, synonym, entity swap, or voice transformations.
claims.notes: Verified from real_benchmark_results.json: 20 bug-exposing cases are all negation type. 30 output-preserving cases (typo, synonym, entity_swap, voice) produce zero violations. Matches Table 7 in tool_paper.tex.

claims.claim: Zero false positives on 30 output-preserving test cases across all four transformation types (typo, synonym, entity swap, voice).
claims.notes: Verified from real_benchmark_results.json metrics: Metamorphic Localizer fp=0, tn=40. No output-preserving transformation caused a false violation detection.

theorems.name: Causal-Differential Fault Localization (M4)
theorems.formal_statement: Given pipeline P = s₁ ∘ ... ∘ sₙ, input x, transformation τ, and end-to-end metamorphic violation, define per-stage differentials Δₖ(x,τ) = dₖ(sₖ(prefixₖ(x)), sₖ(prefixₖ(τ(x)))). Localize to k* = argmax_k [Δₖ − E[Δₖ | τ is meaning-preserving]]. Causal refinement: replace sₖ*'s input with prefixₖ*(x) and re-execute stages k* through n to distinguish introduction from amplification.
theorems.complexity: O(N·n·C_pipeline) for spectrum-based localization, plus O(n·C_pipeline) per violation for interventional refinement.

theorems.name: Stage Discriminability Matrix (N1)
theorems.formal_statement: Define M ∈ ℝⁿˣᵐ where M_{k,j} = E_{x∼G}[Δₖ(x,τⱼ)]. (a) T can localize faults to a unique stage iff rank(M) = n. (b) If rank(M) = r < n, the best achievable localization partitions stages into n−r+1 equivalence classes of indistinguishable stages.
theorems.complexity: Calibration from ~100–200 samples; standard linear algebra (column space argument).

theorems.name: Grammar-Constrained Shrinking Hardness and Convergence (N4a,b,d)
theorems.formal_statement: (a) Finding the globally shortest grammatical counterexample is NP-hard (reduction from Minimum Grammar-Consistent String). (b) GCHDD produces a 1-minimal counterexample in O(|T|²·|R|) feature-checker invocations, each costing O(|F|). (d) Expected shrinking ratio: E[|x'|] ≤ |x|/b + O(α·log|x|) for bounded ambiguity α and branching factor b.
theorems.complexity: O(|T|²·|R|·|F|) worst-case; potentially O(|T|·log|T|·|R|) if binary-search monotonicity lemma holds.

theorems.name: Behavioral Fragility Index (M7)
theorems.formal_statement: BFI(P,τ) = E_x[dist_out(P(x), P(τ(x)))] / E_x[dist_in(x, τ(x))]. BFI >> 1 indicates pipeline amplification; BFI ≈ 1 indicates stability.
theorems.complexity: Estimated empirically from the test corpus. O(N·C_pipeline) for N test inputs.

theorems.name: MR Composition Theorem (M3)
theorems.formal_statement: If transformations τ₁, τ₂ modify disjoint syntactic positions and satisfy task-parameterized meaning preservation independently, then their composition preserves meaning with probability ≥ 1−δ, where δ is empirically bounded via Clopper-Pearson exact binomial confidence intervals. Reduces required test count from O(|T|²) to O(|T|·log|R|) for pairwise coverage.
theorems.complexity: Standard statistical machinery (Clopper-Pearson, union bounds) applied to NLP metamorphic testing.

theorems.name: Causal Introduction vs Amplification (N3-simplified)
theorems.formal_statement: Model the pipeline as a structural causal model. DCE_k(τ) = E[Δₖ | do(S^τ_{k−1} := S_{k−1})]. DCE_k > 0 indicates stage k introduces a fault. IE_k = E[Δₖ] − DCE_k measures fault amplification. DCE_k is always identifiable from a single interventional replay at stage k.
theorems.complexity: O(n·C_pipeline) per violation for interventional replay.

citations.authors: T. Y. Chen, F.-C. Kuo, H. Liu, P.-L. Poon, D. Towey, T. H. Tse, Z. Q. Zhou
citations.title: Metamorphic Testing: A Review of Challenges and Opportunities
citations.relevance: Foundational survey on metamorphic testing. Establishes the theoretical framework for metamorphic relations and their application to testing non-testable programs.

citations.authors: M. T. Ribeiro, T. Wu, C. Guestrin, S. Singh
citations.title: Beyond Accuracy: Behavioral Testing of NLP Models with CheckList
citations.venue: Proceedings of the 58th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL 2020)
citations.relevance: Key prior art for behavioral NLP testing. CheckList detects end-to-end failures but cannot localize to specific pipeline stages — the gap this project fills.

citations.authors: K. Pei, Y. Cao, J. Yang, S. Jana
citations.title: DeepXplore: Automated Whitebox Testing of Deep Learning Systems
citations.venue: Proceedings of the 26th Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP 2017)
citations.relevance: Introduced neuron coverage for DL testing. Motivates coverage-guided testing for neural NLP pipeline stages and provides context for the BFI metric's amplification analysis.

citations.authors: S. Segura, G. Fraser, A. B. Sánchez, A. Ruiz-Cortés
citations.title: A Survey on Metamorphic Testing
citations.relevance: Comprehensive survey of metamorphic testing techniques and applications. Establishes taxonomy of metamorphic relations used to ground the transformation algebra.

citations.authors: W. E. Wong, R. Gao, Y. Li, R. Abreu, F. Wotawa
citations.title: A Survey on Software Fault Localization
citations.relevance: Authoritative survey on fault localization techniques including SBFL. Closest prior art operates on statement-level coverage for Java programs; this project extends to pipeline-stage IR distances for NLP.

citations.title: Simplifying and Isolating Failure-Inducing Input
citations.relevance: Foundational delta debugging algorithm. GCHDD extends this to operate on parse trees with grammaticality and transformation-applicability constraints — a synthesis not in the original work.

citations.authors: J. A. Jones, M. J. Harrold, J. Stasko
citations.title: Visualization of Test Information to Assist Fault Localization
citations.venue: Proceedings of the 24th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE 2002)
citations.relevance: Introduced Tarantula SBFL technique using statement-level pass/fail spectra. Provides baseline SBFL approach that causal-differential localization extends with interventional refinement.

citations.authors: R. Abreu, P. Zoeteweij, A. J. C. van Gemund
citations.title: On the Accuracy of Spectrum-based Fault Localization
citations.venue: Testing: Academic and Industrial Conference Practice and Research Techniques (TAIC PART 2007)
citations.relevance: Introduced the Ochiai coefficient for SBFL and evaluated spectrum-based techniques. The Ochiai coefficient is used as the base SBFL metric before causal refinement in the localizer.

citations.authors: T. Gui, X. Wang, Q. Zhang, B. Qin, M. Ye, D. Zhang, M. Sun
citations.title: TextFlint: Unified Multilingual Robustness Evaluation Toolkit for Natural Language Processing
citations.venue: Proceedings of the 59th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL 2021)
citations.relevance: NLP robustness toolkit with multilingual transformations. Like CheckList, detects end-to-end failures but does not localize to pipeline stages. Catalogues many of the individual transformations this project composes.

citations.authors: D. Jin, Z. Jin, J. T. Zhou, P. Szolovits
citations.title: Is BERT Really Robust? A Strong Baseline for Natural Language Attack and Defense
citations.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI 2020)
citations.relevance: TextFooler adversarial attack baseline. Demonstrates transformer model vulnerabilities to input perturbations, motivating the need for systematic metamorphic testing of NLP pipelines.

citations.authors: K. Goel, N. Rajani, J. Vig, Z. Taber, M. Reese, S. Wu, Y. Sui, T. Worledge, J. Priest, D. Ré, C. Ré
citations.title: Robustness Gym: Unifying the NLP Evaluation Landscape
citations.venue: Proceedings of the 2021 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics (NAACL 2021)
citations.relevance: Unified NLP evaluation framework. Provides holistic pipeline evaluation but without stage-level fault localization or causal analysis capabilities.

citations.title: Combinatorial Aspects of Covering Arrays
citations.relevance: Foundational work on covering arrays used for the product-space coverage optimization (grammar × transformation × MR). Enables the O(|T|·log|R|) test count reduction via the composition theorem.

citations.authors: W. E. Wong, V. Debroy, R. Gao, Y. Li
citations.title: The DStar Method for Effective Software Fault Localization
citations.relevance: DStar SBFL coefficient. One of the most effective fault localization formulas, providing context for the modified Ochiai approach used in the localizer.

citations.authors: K. Simonyan, A. Vedaldi, A. Zisserman
citations.title: Deep Inside Convolutional Networks: Visualising Image Classification Models and Saliency Maps
citations.relevance: Gradient-based saliency for neural networks. Used as the baseline for gradient-approximation attribution in the evaluation (leave-one-out word ablation approximating gradient saliency).

citations.authors: T. Y. Chen, S. C. Cheung, S. M. Yiu
citations.title: Metamorphic Testing: A New Approach for Generating Next Test Cases
citations.venue: Technical Report HKUST-CS98-01, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
citations.relevance: Foundational work on metamorphic testing. Introduced the concept of metamorphic relations for testing non-testable programs. Cited in tool_paper.tex as the origin of metamorphic testing.

code_map.description: Core fault localization engine — orchestrates spectrum-based localization with causal refinement for pipeline-stage fault identification.
Direct Causal Effect (DCE) and Indirect Effect (IE) computation — implements the introduction-vs-amplification decomposition via interventional replay.
Interventional analysis — replaces stage k's output with original execution output and observes downstream behavior change.
Grammar-Constrained Hierarchical Delta Debugging (GCHDD) — parse-tree delta debugging with grammaticality and transformation-applicability constraints.
Core delta debugging algorithm extended for grammar-aware operation with convergence bound O(|T|²·|R|).
1-minimality checking — verifies no single grammar-valid subtree replacement further reduces the counterexample.
Stage discriminability matrix computation — M_{k,j} = E_{x∼G}[Δₖ(x,τⱼ)] with rank analysis for diagnostic completeness.
Matrix operations for discriminability analysis — rank computation, equivalence class identification for indistinguishable stages.
Behavioral Fragility Index computation — BFI(P,τ) = E_x[dist_out]/E_x[dist_in] with task-specific distance functions.
Report summary generation — includes BFI values, per-stage fragility rankings, and cross-pipeline comparisons.
Transformation composition and MR composition theorem — tracks which transformation pairs commute and which compositions preserve semantic predicates.
Coverage analyzer — tracks grammar × transformation × MR pairwise coverage and implements composition-theorem-based test count optimization.
Passivization transformation — auxiliary 'be' insertion, object-to-subject promotion, by-PP demotion, ditransitive handling.
Clefting transformation — it-cleft construction with focus element extraction.
There-insertion transformation — existential construction with definiteness and unaccusative/copular verb constraints.
Dative alternation transformation — double-object ↔ prepositional dative conversion.
Transformation registry — manages all 15 core transformations with metadata on preservation scope per NLP task.
Spectrum-based fault localization — implements Ochiai, DStar, Barinel, and Tarantula coefficients for baseline SBFL.
Suspiciousness score computation — per-stage suspiciousness ranking used as input to causal refinement.
Parse tree operations for shrinking — subtree enumeration, replacement, and derivation-tree pruning.
Causal localization driver — orchestrates interventional replay, DCE/IE computation, and iterative multi-fault peeling.
Feature-unification validity checker — deterministic oracle for grammaticality checking during shrinking. Answers 'is this subtree replacement grammatical?' in O(|R|) time.
CLI entry point — user-facing tool for configuring pipeline topology, running test suites, and generating structured compliance reports for regulated industries.
Test selector — adaptive multi-armed bandit allocation for budget-constrained execution, prioritizing high-value tests within user-specified CPU budgets.
Evaluation harness — 5-tier evaluation infrastructure including fault injection, localization accuracy measurement, and GPT-4-as-debugger baseline comparison.
Automated metamorphic relation learner: specification mining via probing, delta clustering, and MR extraction across T5, LLaMA, and CLIP-style architectures.
Empirical completeness validator — fault-injection sweeps, KL factorization checks, bounds comparison, and shared-encoder conditional-MI fallback for N2 robustness.
